key_insight: Separating physicochemical descriptors and molecular graph structure into two additive branches makes aqueous solubility predictions more interpretable by showing what comes from chemistry versus topology.
clinical_relevance: More transparent solubility prediction can help early drug discovery prioritize compounds with better developability, though it does not by itself determine clinical suitability.
method: Chemical branch: multilayer perceptron encodes physicochemical descriptors
Structural branch: graph neural network encodes molecular graph topology
Outputs are combined additively, with an optional multiplicative interaction
evidence_signal: Pretraining on AqSolDB and fine-tuning on BigSolDB2 improved accuracy and reduced run-to-run variability, with competitive performance across both datasets.
watch_item: Interpretability is model-based and dataset-dependent, so the decomposition may not fully generalize to other chemistry spaces or experimental solubility conditions.
paper_title: An Additive MLP-GNN Framework for Characterizing Chemical and Structural Contributions to Aqueous Solubility
